content.problemStatement:
Given integers NNN and MMM, find the number of ordered pairs (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) such that 1≤a<b≤N1 \le a < b \le N1≤a<b≤N and ((M mod a) mod b)=((M mod b) mod a)((M\ \mathrm{mod}\ a)\ \mathrm{mod}\ b) = ((M\ \mathrm{mod}\ b)\ \mathrm{mod}\ a)((M mod a) mod b)=((M mod b) mod a).

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains two integers NNN, MMM.
###Output
For each testcase, output in a single line the answer to the problem.

###Constraints


1≤T≤10001 \leq T \leq 10001≤T≤1000
2≤N≤1062 \leq N \leq 10^62≤N≤106
1≤M≤5⋅1051 \leq M \leq 5\cdot 10^51≤M≤5⋅105
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
**Note:** Multiplier for JAVA for this problem is reduced to 1.251.251.25 instead of usual 222.

###Subtasks
**Subtask #1 (10 points):**


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
2≤N≤1032 \leq N \leq 10^32≤N≤103
1≤M≤1051 \leq M \leq 10^51≤M≤105
**Subtask #2 (40 points):**


1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
2≤N≤1052 \leq N \leq 10^52≤N≤105
1≤M≤1051 \leq M \leq 10^51≤M≤105
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
